assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been recognised as a receivable before settlement.
The question concerns accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognised assets or liabilities as possible hedged items, making fair value hedge accounting a distinct top-level hedge accounting model relevant to foreign currency risk on a recognised receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats forecast transactions, including certain highly probable intragroup foreign currency transactions in consolidation, within its hedge accounting framework, so cash flow hedge accounting is a distinct peer model relevant to the issue area.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRS 9, IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 guidance on hedges of foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations, which is a separate top-level hedge accounting model relevant to foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements.
